Toss Update: Toss belongs to  as they decide to chase in the TATA IPL 2026 opener! 



Pitch Report by Morrison and Pietersen:
With a touch of cloud cover hovering but conditions largely ideal, the M Chinnaswamy Stadium once again sets up as a paradise for batters. The boundaries are compact, around 64 to 65 metres square and just 73 metres straight, offering little breathing space for bowlers. The surface looks good for stroke play, and while a few bare areas can be spotted, they are not expected to alter proceedings significantly. Considering the strength, bat speed and modern power games of today’s players, this ground feels even smaller. Once batters settle in, runs should flow freely, and with conditions holding steady, winning the toss and opting to chase could be the smart call tonight.



The Big Battle to Watch Out For:

Virat Kohli versus Harshal Patel could well shape the tone of the evening. Across six innings, Kohli has muscled 80 runs from just 47 balls at a strike rate north of 170, but Harshal has had his say too, dismissing the talisman twice when it mattered.


Hello and welcome to our live coverage of the IPL 2026 opener as the two sides from the unforgettable 2016 final begin their new campaigns tonight. Toss updates, pitch reports, and team news are on the way. Until then, soak in our match preview below.

The curtains rise on TATA IPL 2026 with a fixture soaked in story and sound as Royal Challengers Bengaluru  begin their title defence against familiar headaches Sunrisers Hyderabad  at M Chinnaswamy Stadium on 28 March. and the champions know that opening nights can define moods long before they define tables. Yet every RCB fan also knows that when SRH arrive, history refuses to stay silent.


Bengaluru’s build up carries pride tempered with concern as Josh Hazelwood continues his recovery, pushing Rajat Patidar and his men toward early adaptability. All eyes turn to the opening pair of Virat Kohli and Phil Salt, a combination carved for velocity and intent on short boundaries. A fast start is not a luxury here, it is a requirement, with the middle order itching to turn belief into dominance in front of their own people.


Sunrisers Hyderabad land with a refreshed squad and an intriguing leadership subplot. Ishan Kishan steps in as captain with Pat Cummins set to join later, bringing urgency and aggression to the role. An Abhishek Sharma and Travis Head opening burst threatens to hush crowds quickly, and SRH have shown before that Bengaluru’s noise fuels them rather than frightens them.


The Harshal Patel and Bhuvneshwar Kumar duel adds poetic spice to the night. Harshal won his Purple Cap wearing RCB colours and now returns in orange aiming to haunt his former home, while Bhuvneshwar, once SRH’s Purple Cap hero, now wears red as he looks to undo the side he once led with the ball. Experience, movement and nerve will matter most under lights.


Season openers thrive on chaos and courage. Champions against challengers,  belief against memory. Somewhere between a roaring crowd and a swinging ball, IPL 2026 will announce itself, loudly and without apology.
 

Head to Head In IPL:

History slightly favours the men in orange whenever these two sides collide. Across 25 fiercely fought IPL encounters, Sunrisers Hyderabad have emerged victorious 14 times, a tally that includes one dramatic Super Over finish. Royal Challengers Bengaluru follow closely with 11 wins, proof that margins have often been slim. While RCB now carry champion swagger, past numbers whisper that SRH know how to make this rivalry uncomfortable, especially when pressure is highest.



 

Venue at a Glance

M Chinnaswamy Stadium continues to live up to its reputation as a balanced yet explosive venue. Over the last two seasons, 12 matches have been played here with teams batting first and second winning an equal six games each, underlining the absence of a clear toss advantage.

The average first innings score stands at a healthy 186, driven largely by frenetic powerplays that have produced an average of 56 runs. Once the game slips into the final overs, bowlers have found little mercy, conceding at a sharp run rate of 11.4 in the death phase.
Fast bowlers have still found a way to influence proceedings, accounting for 75 percent of the wickets, while spinners have chipped in with the remaining 25 percent, usually when batters gamble for glory.
MatchSummary.PostMatchCommentary:
Jacob Duffy (Player of the Match):
"It was a great experience playing my first game here because I had heard so much about RCB and it was quite a way to begin the campaign. I was just keeping the big fella’s seat warm and felt lucky that the captain kept trusting me with the ball which meant my work finished early. Bowling to Ishan Kishan and Abhishek is never easy and this surface is not very friendly for bowlers but I am learning quickly. Rajat simply told me to go ahead and bowl when the ball was hard because that is the best time to make an impact. Overall it was a special start and I really enjoyed the challenge."
 

Flying start to our very own Kiwi, Jacob Duffy. ?????????

POTM on his IPL debut. ??

— Royal Challengers Bengaluru (@RCBTweets) March 28, 2026

Rajat Patidar (Royal Challengers Bengaluru Captain):
"It was a perfect start to the season and I am very happy with the way the boys performed in the opening game. Duffy was outstanding in his first match and picking up key wickets early made a big difference while Abhinandan also impressed on debut. There are no words for the way our main batter keeps finishing games because he is consistently there till the end and makes things happen. The catch from Phil Salt was terrific and he is an exceptional fielder who produces moments like that regularly. I tried to add as much impact as possible with the bat and that intent will stay throughout the season."

 

Ishan Kishan (Sunrisers Hyderabad Captain):
"The wicket settled down after the first few overs but losing early wickets meant we had to be careful and assess things better. It played even better in the second innings so we need to be smarter with our shot selection going forward. Credit has to go to the opposition batters, especially Virat bhai, because getting his wicket early is always crucial. These mistakes are understandable in the first game, but we need to learn quickly and improve as a bowling unit. I really enjoyed the captaincy experience and while there are nerves in the IPL if we keep winning games it will set us up well for the rest of the tournament."



Royal Challengers Bengaluru  produced a statement chase to outclass Sunrisers Hyderabad , transforming a demanding target into an exhibition of control, confidence and depth.

Sunrisers Hyderabad had earlier leaned heavily on Ishan Kishan, whose commanding eighty off thirty-eight balls rescued the innings after early setbacks. Kishan dictated the middle overs with authority, combining clean striking with smart placement as wickets fell around him. Aniket Verma then provided a late surge with a ferocious forty three, ensuring Hyderabad crossed the two-hundred mark and kept the contest alive.

Bengaluru’s response was swift and decisive. Devdutt Padikkal set the tone with an elegant yet aggressive sixty one off twenty six deliveries, tearing into the powerplay and shifting momentum permanently. His timing was pure and his intent unmistakable, forcing the bowlers off their plans.

Virat Kohli followed with a composed unbeaten sixty nine, anchoring the chase with experience and assurance while allowing others to play freely around him. Rajat Patidar contributed a sharp and impactful cameo, and Tim David calmly applied the finishing touches.

The target was reached with ease and time to spare, as Bengaluru delivered a performance that stood as a confident reminder of their batting strength rather than merely an afterthought to Hyderabad’s effort.
